campbell.title: Campbell City Council — May 5, 2026
campbell.summary: The Campbell City Council held a meeting where they reviewed financial investments for March 2026 with help from the regional transit authority. The council also adopted a new policy limiting how the city's buildings and property can be used by federal agencies, and discussed efforts to fill open city jobs and keep current employees.
keyTopics: March 2026 investment and financial transactions report
New policy restricting federal agency use of city property
City job openings and employee retention strategies

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — April 15, 2026
saratoga.summary: The City Council held a hearing where residents could object to a plan to clean up hazardous materials on certain properties in Saratoga that the County had identified. The Council also adopted new policies about how the city handles technology changes and how to help residents who need special accommodations to access city services.
keyTopics: Hazardous waste cleanup on local properties
New technology disruption policy for the city
Process for requesting reasonable accommodations from the city

los-gatos.title: Los Gatos City Council — May 5, 2026
los-gatos.summary: The Los Gatos City Council met on May 5, 2026, to approve several routine matters including meeting minutes and a new rule declaring overgrown brush and vegetation a public nuisance that the city can require property owners to clear. The council also approved engineer reports for landscape and lighting work and authorized the town manager to sign multi-year service contracts with Pacific and other vendors.
keyTopics: Hazardous vegetation/brush declared a public nuisance - property owners may be required to clear it
Landscape and lighting improvements approved with engineer's report
Five-year service agreement authorized
Multiple contract renewals and amendments approved for town services

san-jose.title: San José City Council — May 13, 2026
san-jose.summary: The San José City Council met to organize their upcoming meetings and schedules. They reviewed what items would be on the May 19th meeting agenda, decided which items needed specific time slots, and set up the council's meeting schedule for the next fiscal year.
keyTopics: May 19, 2026 meeting agenda finalization
City Council meeting schedule for fiscal year 2026-2027
Appointments to city committees, boards, and commissions
Council member travel requests

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — May 14, 2026
mountain-view.summary: The Mountain View City Council is inviting residents to submit questions before their May 14, 2026 meeting. The council will be discussing various boards, commissions, and committees, with specific meeting locations posted at least 24 hours in advance.
keyTopics: Submit public questions online before the meeting
Board, commission, and committee agendas and locations
Staff reports available at City Clerk's Office (500 Castro Street)
Contact City Manager's Office with agenda questions (650-903-6301 or CNC@MountainView.gov)

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — May 5, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The City Council held a special closed-door meeting at 5 P.M. to discuss confidential matters before reconvening for a regular public meeting at 7 P.M. The agenda included a flag salute and what appears to be a focus on small business issues, though specific details from the closed session weren't disclosed to the public.
keyTopics: Closed session meeting before the public agenda
Small business agenda items
Regular council meeting reconvened at 7 P.M.

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — May 11, 2026
cupertino.summary: The Cupertino City Council held an initial review session for the city's budget plan for fiscal year 2026-27 to discuss spending priorities and financial plans. The council also covered rules requiring people who try to influence city decisions on specific issues to register as lobbyists and report their activities.
keyTopics: FY 2026-27 budget proposal review and discussion
City spending priorities and financial planning
Lobbyist registration and disclosure requirements
Rules for people attempting to influence city decisions

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — May 14, 2026
santa-clara.summary: The Santa Clara City Council meeting on May 14, 2026 included important legal information for residents about how long you have to challenge city decisions in court. If the city makes an official decision that affects you, you have 90 days from when that decision becomes final to file a lawsuit or legal challenge, unless state law says otherwise.
keyTopics: 90-day deadline to sue the city over official decisions
Legal challenge procedures for quasi-adjudicative decisions
State law governs lawsuit time limits (California Code of Civil Procedure Section 1094.6)

milpitas.title: Milpitas City Council — May 5, 2026
milpitas.summary: The Milpitas City Council held a meeting to discuss the city's financial plans for the next several years, including a 5-year capital improvement plan and next year's operating budget. They also approved a new master plan for parks and trails in the Innovation District and made updates to how the council conducts its meetings.
keyTopics: 5-year capital projects plan (2027-2031) - what infrastructure and improvements the city will fund
2026-27 operating budget presentation - how the city will spend money on day-to-day operations
Innovation District Parks and Trails Master Plan - new parks and walking/biking trails coming to the area
City Council procedures updates - changes to how meetings are run

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — May 7, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The City Council held a hearing about a plan to build a 6-story apartment building with 72 units at 450 Lytton Avenue, replacing a city parking lot. All units would be affordable housing for low-income residents, and the developer is asking for special approvals and waivers to make the project work.
keyTopics: 72 affordable housing units on a small 0.43-acre lot
Replacing 52-space city parking lot (Lot T)
Six-story building with streamlined approval process
Request for density bonus concessions under state law
Environmental review underway to determine if exemption applies
